title: Bobea gaudichaudii
intro: Bobea gaudichaudii, also known as the Ma'o hau hele, is a flowering plant native to the Hawaiian Islands, belonging to the family Rubiaceae. This plant has been used in traditional Hawaiian medicine for its potential health benefits, including the treatment of fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Bobea gaudichaudii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Hawaiian herbalism, Bobea gaudichaudii is often prepared as a tea or infusion, and is also used to make a tincture or salve, which are used to treat various health conditions.
